stories.headline: Newsday uncovers NYC tax, billing mess
stories.summary: Penny Loeb's investigation revealed widespread errors in NYC's tax and billing system, affecting numerous residents with incorrect or missing bills.

stories.headline: Special interest contributions revealed
stories.summary: An investigation into Boston Mayor Raymond Flynn's campaign finances revealed that nearly 90 percent of his donors from 1987-89 had direct financial ties to City Hall, including city employees, contractors, and developers, raising concerns about potential conflicts of interest as major donors like Fidelity Investments executives gave contributions while negotiating city projects.

stories.headline: Press-Enterprise exposes tax inequities
stories.summary: A Riverside Press Enterprise reporter used computer-assisted research to document how California's Proposition 13 creates massive property tax disparities between homeowners, with some paying 17 times more than neighbors with comparable properties depending on when their homes were purchased.
